summary: Cerebras Systems Inc. shares jumped 68% in its trading debut after raising $5.5 billion in the year’s largest IPO, illustrating investors’ surging appetite for artificial intelligence data centers and the chips running them.

summary: The Trump administration pledged $1.8 billion for the United Nations’ humanitarian arm and suggested more funds could be on the way, even as the White House withholds money to pressure the global organization over its priorities.

summary: Three of the first venture capital investors in Cerebras Systems Inc. are set to earn billions of dollars from their early bets on the chipmaker following its Wall Street debut on Thursday. Benchmark will own roughly 8.1% of the outstanding shares in Cerebras after the initial public offering, according to a securities filing. title: Macro Matters: NISA Investments’ Douglass on Fed, Fiscal Outlook
summary: Stephen Douglass, chief economist and a member of NISA Investment Advisors’ global investment committee, explains why he still sees the US economy as broadly consistent with a soft-landing path once the current oil shock fades, even though the war has complicated the timing of Fed rate cuts. He joined Ira Jersey, Bloomberg Intelligence’s chief US interest-rate strategist on this edition of Macro Matters. title: Oil Heads for Weekly Advance With Iran War Resolution at Impasse
summary: Oil headed for a weekly gain as the crucial Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed, with efforts to end the war in limbo and disruptions that have upended global markets set to linger.

title: Gautam Adani Agrees to Pay $6 Million to Settle SEC Fraud Case
summary: Gautam Adani agreed to pay $6 million to settle Securities and Exchange Commission allegations that he broke US securities rules by making false and misleading representations about Adani Green Energy Ltd.

title: Xi Cements Taiwan as Top US-China Risk
summary: Xi Jinping warned that Taiwan could lead to "clashes" between the US and China, calling it a "highly dangerous situation" for the world's biggest economies. Bloomberg's Stephen Engle breaks down the situation. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Heads for Weekly Drop as Inflation Fuels Rate-Hike Bets
summary: Gold headed for a modest weekly decline as a war-driven surge in US inflation fueled expectations for higher interest rates.

title: Ohio Governor Candidate Ramaswamy Sells All His BuzzFeed Shares
summary: Vivek Ramaswamy, the biotech entrepreneur who won the Republican Party’s nomination for Ohio governor this month, has sold off all his shares in BuzzFeed Inc.

title: Asian Stocks to Rise as AI Rally Spurs US Gauges: Markets Wrap
summary: Asian stocks were set to rise Friday after Wall Street gauges hit records, buoyed by a rally in the AI trade, strong corporate earnings and further signs of resilience in the US consumer.

title: PE Firm Lightrock Raises $500 Million for New Clean Energy Fund
summary: Private equity firm Lightrock, which was spun out from Liechtenstein’s LGT Group, has raised a new $500 million fund to back businesses advancing access to affordable and clean energy in Asia and Africa.

title: Chinese Chipmakers’ Lofty Valuations Prompt Investor Caution
summary: A recent surge in Chinese chip stocks has made them some of the most expensive among global peers, and investors are growing increasingly cautious.

title: Apollo’s West Technology Is in Talks to Sell Remaining Business
summary: Apollo Global Management Inc.-backed West Technology is engaged in exclusive talks to sell its remaining operating business to a counter-party, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Foreign Investors Fear Retreat in Japan’s Governance Reforms
summary: Investor concern is mounting over a possible rollback of Japan’s corporate governance reforms, a key driver of foreign investment, threatening a rally that pushed equities to record levels.

title: S&P 500 Tops 7,500 as AI Fuels Record-Breaking Run: Markets Wrap
summary: A revival of the artificial-intelligence trade kept fueling momentum in stocks, with the market also rising after retail sales showed signs of consumer strength despite a war-driven surge in energy costs.

title: Pound Tumbles as Burnham Escalates Labour Revolt Against Starmer
summary: A potential challenge to UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s leadership from one of the nation’s most popular Labour Party politicians sent the pound tumbling by casting doubt on the future of his government and its efforts to rein in the national debt.

title: Private Aviation 'Immunized' from Fuel Cost Spikes, Says Wheels Up's George Mattson
summary:
George Mattson, CEO of private aviation company Wheels Up, said that his company is 'immunized' from the impact of fuel cost spikes because they are able to pass off surcharges to customers willing to pay a premium. 
Mattson discussed Delta's investment in the private sphere and said the airline company sees Wheels Up as an extension of its growing premium commercial strategy.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Winklevosses Invest $100 Million in Battered Gemini Exchange
summary: Tyler and Cameron Winklevoss, the billionaire twins who founded Gemini Space Station Inc., made a $100 million “strategic investment” into the money-losing crypto exchange.

title: World Bank Team to Visit Venezuela to Meet Government Officials
summary: A group of representatives from the World Bank will visit Caracas to engage with government authorities for the first time since reestablishing formal relations last month, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Why Rare Earths Are China’s Trump Card in Trade War
summary: Rare earths are among the most critical raw materials on the planet, deeply embedded in the technologies that underpin modern life — from iPhones and cars to data centers and MRI machines.

title: Blackstone, KKR to Take Over Dental Firm After Slashing 70% Debt
summary: Direct lenders including Blackstone Inc. and KKR & Co. are set to take control of struggling dental business Affordable Care and slash about 70% of the debt as part of a restructuring agreement, according to a person familiar with the matter.

title: Emerging Currencies Dip as US Retail Data Boosts Dollar
summary: Most emerging-market currencies fell Thursday on the back of a stronger dollar after US retail sales signaled consumer resilience in the world’s largest economy despite sharply higher gasoline prices.

title: Gold Declines as Market Weighs Federal Reserve Rate Path
summary: Gold fell as investors weighed the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate path after US data this week showed a war-driven surge in inflation.

title: Red Hot Demand for Risky US Dollar Loans Brings Bigger Deals
summary: Firms are borrowing more than expected in the US leveraged loan market as they take advantage of strong investor appetite for riskier debt.

title: AI Rally Crushes Stock Pickers With Just 1 in 4 Beating Market
summary: Active managers who briefly looked like they might finally have their moment earlier this year are once again confronting a familiar problem: a market rally driven by a tiny group of tech megacaps that diversified portfolios simply can’t keep up with.

title: Andrew Left’s Short Reports ‘Scared’ Everyday Investors
summary: The impact of short selling on every day investors was highlighted by prosecutors on Thursday at Citron Research founder Andrew Left’s criminal trial.

title: Cerebras’ IPO Price Soars | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Cerebras CEO Is Worth $3.2 Billion After Year’s Largest IPO
summary: Serial Silicon Valley entrepreneur Andrew Feldman, who literally grew up on the campus of Stanford University, was already involved in selling three companies and taking another public. But none of that compares with Thursday’s debut of his AI-chip manufacturer.

title: AI Mega IPO, Cisco Results Propel US Stocks to Another Milestone
summary: The artificial intelligence trade kept firing up US equities to fresh records after upbeat earnings from Cisco Systems Inc. and a landmark AI IPO.

title: Kushner’s Thrive Capital Invests $100 Million in Shopify
summary: Joshua Kushner’s Thrive Capital has taken a stake in Shopify Inc., according to people familiar with the matter, marking a rare bet by the venture firm on a public company.

title: Hantavirus Still Has World Worried, No More Cases in US
summary: There are no known cases of anyone with the hantavirus in the US now according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. 41 people exposed while on the Hondius cruise ship are being monitored. Dr. Amesh Adalja, senior scholar at the Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security, joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How Epstein Built His Sprawling Web of Influence
summary: Jeffrey Epstein’s emails, appointment books and message logs reveal how the disgraced financier built a sprawling web of rich, powerful and influential contacts. He was a relentless networker, regularly introducing his acquaintances to each other and treating those introductions like business deals — always putting himself at the center of the transaction. Bloomberg's Lizzie Fournier joins to discuss.  (Source: Bloomberg)
